questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Your water filter produces 2L/hour. For 4 people needing 2L/day each, how many hours must it run?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: You have 24 coconuts shared equally among 4 people. How many does each person get?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A campfire burns 3 logs per hour. How many logs do you need for 8 hours?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: If you walk 4 km/h and the water source is 6 km away, how long is the round trip?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: You caught 15 fish over 3 days. What is the average catch per day?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Your shelter needs 8 bamboo poles. Each pole takes 10 minutes to cut. Total cutting time?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A rope is 12 meters long. You need 4 equal pieces. How long is each piece?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Your food supply lasts 10 days for 2 people. How many days will it last for 4 people?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: If the temperature drops 2 degrees per hour, how much colder is it after 5 hours?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: You collect 500 mL of rainwater per hour. How many hours to fill a 2-liter container?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A solar still produces 0.75L of water per day. How many days to produce 6L?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Your shelter wall is 3m wide and 2m tall. What is the surface area in square meters?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Wind speed is 80 km/h. Convert to m/s (divide by 3.6).

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: You need 6 teaspoons of sugar per liter for rehydration solution. For 3 liters, how many teaspoons?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A rescue plane travels at 200 km/h. If it is 50 km away, how many minutes until it reaches you?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Each person in your team needs 2000 calories/day. For 4 people over 3 days, total calories needed?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A signal flare reaches 200m height. If it rises at 40 m/s, how many seconds to reach max height?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: You have 120 material units. Building costs 15 per shelter wall and you need 4 walls. Units remaining?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Mixing sealant: 2 parts resin to 1 part hardener. If you use 40 mL resin, how much hardener?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A battery provides 9V across 150 ohms. Using V=IR, what current flows? (in amps)

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Wind force F = 0.5 x 1.225 x v^2 x A. With v=22 m/s and A=6 m^2, what is F (approx)?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Underwater pressure = 1 + (depth/10) atm. At 250m depth, what is the pressure?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Power P = V^2/R. With V=12V and R=100 ohms, what power in watts?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A triangular roof has base 4m and height 2m. What is its area?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Temperature drops from 32C to 18C over 7 hours. Average rate of change per hour?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Signal strength decays by half for every 2 km. Starting at 100 units, what strength at 6 km?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Your raft travels at 3 km/h against a 1.5 km/h current. Net speed and time to go 6 km?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: An escape pod re-enters at 6.5 degrees. The entry corridor is 5.5 to 7.5 degrees. What is the margin of error on each side?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A buoy has 509 N upward force. Adding 26 kg ballast (g=9.8), what is the new net force?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Fuel consumption is 0.5 kg/min. Thruster burn lasts 2 minutes. Starting with 5 kg fuel, how much remains?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A thermoelectric generator produces 50W per 100C difference. Vent at 350C, ocean at 2C, TEG is 10m away where temp is 200C. Power output?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: 4 people produce 1 kg CO2 each per day. A scrubber canister absorbs 1 kg. For 5 days, how many canisters minimum?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Resource equity: team of 4 gets food shares of 30, 25, 25, 20 (total 100). The Gini coefficient measures inequality. Which distribution is most equitable?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A parabolic reflector doubles signal strength. If base signal is 0.54W, reflected is 1.08W. Over 5 km, signal drops to 1/(d^2). Strength at 5 km?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Pressure at 1500m: 151 atm. Force on a 0.5 m^2 viewport (1 atm = 101325 Pa): F = P x A in meganewtons (MN)?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Solar panel tilt at angle A reduces power by cos(A). At 45 degrees (cos45=0.707), what percentage of max power?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Chemical reaction rate doubles per 10C rise. At baseline 20C it takes 60 min. How long at 40C?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A communication window lasts 4 minutes. Signal delay is 1.2 seconds round-trip. Max full exchanges (question+answer)?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Hull crack leaks 0.5% air/hour. Starting at 100%, how many hours until 50% remains? (exponential decay)

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: 3 tilted solar panels at 30 degrees during 10x flare: each produces 200 x 10 x cos(30) = 1732W. Total for 3 panels?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Orbital velocity v = sqrt(GM/r). With GM=3.986e14, r=6.771e6 m (400km orbit), find v in km/s.

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Buoyancy: object volume 0.08 m^3 in seawater (1025 kg/m^3). Buoyant force = rho x V x g. Subtract weight (30kg x 9.8). Net force?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Re-entry heating: Q = 0.5 x rho x v^3 x Cd x A. With v=7800 m/s, simplified peak heating rate is ~1.5 MW/m^2. Heat shield area 3 m^2. Total heat load in MW?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Resource optimization: 100 units split among 4 needs (food, water, shelter, signal). Minimum per category is 15. Remaining 40 units maximize signal. Best signal allocation?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Compound interest on bacterial growth: colony doubles every 4 hours. Starting at 100, population after 24 hours?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: A rescue signal must reach -90 dBm. Transmit at -110 dBm, +15 dB amp, +8 dB dish, -5 dB cable loss. Final strength?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Oxygen: 4 people consume 0.84 kg O2/day each. Reserve: 20 kg. How many days of breathable air?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Water desalination: 35000 ppm seawater through 99.5% RO membrane. Output ppm? Is it safe (<500 ppm)?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Parachute deployment: pod falls at 200 m/s at 3000m. Chute slows to 5 m/s in 10 seconds. Average deceleration in m/s^2?

questions.subject: applied_math
questions.text: Team communication efficiency: 48 messages sent, 32 were on-task, 12 shared unique info. Info share rate and on-task ratio?
